content.text: Good. I'll now deploy all the necessary resources: a headless Service for inter-node discovery, the `es-http` Service for external HTTPS access, a ConfigMap for Elasticsearch config, and a 3-node StatefulSet.

input.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
---
# Headless service for StatefulSet pod DNS (internal cluster transport)
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-transport
  namespace: elasticsearch
  labels:
    app: elasticsearch
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
    - name: transport
      port: 9300
      targetPort: 9300
---
# Client-facing HTTPS service
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-http
  namespace: elasticsearch
  labels:
    app: elasticsearch
spec:
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
    - name: https
      port: 9200
      targetPort: 9200
---
# Elasticsearch configuration
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: es-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  elasticsearch.yml: |
    cluster.name: "es-cluster"
    network.host: 0.0.0.0

    # Discovery
    discovery.seed_hosts:
      - es-transport-0.es-transport.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
      - es-transport-1.es-transport.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
      - es-transport-2.es-transport.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
    cluster.initial_master_nodes:
      - es-transport-0
      - es-transport-1
      - es-transport-2

    # TLS for HTTP (port 9200)
    xpack.security.enabled: true
    xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.http.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt

    # TLS for transport (port 9300) — use same cert for simplicity
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
EOF

input.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: es-transport
  namespace: elasticsearch
  labels:
    app: elasticsearch
spec:
  serviceName: es-transport
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: elasticsearch
    spec:
      initContainers:
        - name: fix-permissions
          image: busybox:1.35
          command: ["sh", "-c", "chown -R 1000:1000 /usr/share/elasticsearch/data"]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
        - name: increase-vm-max-map
          image: busybox:1.35
          command: ["sysctl", "-w", "vm.max_map_count=262144"]
          securityContext:
            privileged: true
      containers:
        - name: elasticsearch
          image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
          env:
            - name: node.name
              valueFrom:
                fieldRef:
                  fieldPath: metadata.name
            - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: elastic-password
                  key: password
            - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
              value: "-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
          ports:
            - containerPort: 9200
              name: https
            - containerPort: 9300
              name: transport
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
            - name: config
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
              subPath: elasticsearch.yml
            - name: certs
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs
              readOnly: true
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command:
                - sh
                - -c
                - |
                  curl -sk -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}" \
                    https://localhost:9200/_cluster/health \
                    | grep -qE '"status":"(green|yellow)"'
            initialDelaySeconds: 30
            periodSeconds: 10
            failureThreshold: 12
          resources:
            requests:
              memory: "1Gi"
              cpu: "500m"
            limits:
              memory: "2Gi"
              cpu: "1"
      volumes:
        - name: config
          configMap:
            name: es-config
        - name: certs
          secret:
            secretName: es-http-tls
  volumeClaimTemplates:
    - metadata:
        name: data
      spec:
        accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
        resources:
          requests:
            storage: 5Gi
EOF

for i in $(seq 1 60); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl get pods -n elasticsearch -l app=elasticsearch --no-headers 2>/dev/null)
  echo "--- attempt $i ---"
  echo "$STATUS"
  READY=$(echo "$STATUS" | grep -c "1/1" || true)
  if [ "$READY" -eq 3 ]; then
    echo "All 3 pods Ready!"
    break
  fi
  sleep 15
done

content.thinking:
The pod is running but only pod 0 is up. The StatefulSet creates pods in order, so pod 1 and 2 need to be created. Let me check if there's a pending PVC for pod 1 and 2, or if the StatefulSet is waiting for pod 0 to be ready before creating pod 1.

The issue is that StatefulSet creates pods one at a time in order, waiting for each to be ready before creating the next. Since pod 0's readiness probe is timing out (probably because the cluster hasn't formed yet with only one node, so cluster health might not be green/yellow immediately during startup), this is blocking pods 1 and 2 from being created.
The readiness probe timeout is set to just 1 second by default, which is too short for the curl command to complete. I can either switch the StatefulSet to parallel pod management so all three start at once, increase the probe timeout, or do both. Let me patch it to use parallel management and fix the timeout.

Since `podManagementPolicy` is immutable on StatefulSets, I'll need to delete and recreate it rather than patch it in place.

content.text: The StatefulSet's default `OrderedReady` policy is blocking: pod-0 can't become Ready without nodes 1 & 2, which won't start until pod-0 is Ready. I need to use `Parallel` pod management and fix the readiness probe timeout (1s is too short for curl).

input.command: kubectl delete statefulset es-transport -n elasticsearch --cascade=orphan 2>&1

input.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: es-transport
  namespace: elasticsearch
  labels:
    app: elasticsearch
spec:
  serviceName: es-transport
  replicas: 3
  podManagementPolicy: Parallel
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: elasticsearch
    spec:
      initContainers:
        - name: fix-permissions
          image: busybox:1.35
          command: ["sh", "-c", "chown -R 1000:1000 /usr/share/elasticsearch/data"]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
        - name: increase-vm-max-map
          image: busybox:1.35
          command: ["sysctl", "-w", "vm.max_map_count=262144"]
          securityContext:
            privileged: true
      containers:
        - name: elasticsearch
          image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
          env:
            - name: node.name
              valueFrom:
                fieldRef:
                  fieldPath: metadata.name
            - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: elastic-password
                  key: password
            - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
              value: "-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
          ports:
            - containerPort: 9200
              name: https
            - containerPort: 9300
              name: transport
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
            - name: config
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
              subPath: elasticsearch.yml
            - name: certs
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs
              readOnly: true
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command:
                - sh
                - -c
                - |
                  curl -sk --max-time 5 -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}" \
                    https://localhost:9200/_cluster/health \
                    | grep -qE '"status":"(green|yellow)"'
            initialDelaySeconds: 60
            periodSeconds: 15
            timeoutSeconds: 10
            failureThreshold: 20
          resources:
            requests:
              memory: "1Gi"
              cpu: "500m"
            limits:
              memory: "2Gi"
              cpu: "1"
      volumes:
        - name: config
          configMap:
            name: es-config
        - name: certs
          secret:
            secretName: es-http-tls
  volumeClaimTemplates:
    - metadata:
        name: data
      spec:
        accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
        resources:
          requests:
            storage: 5Gi
EOF
